statement.markdown: Solve the problem and fix the bugs.
statement.inputFormat: A singly linked list is given as a list of integers.
statement.outputFormat: Return the modified linked list where duplicates are removed.
statement.notes: You are allowed to modify the linked list in place.

hints.text: Use a HashSet to track unique elements

editorial.title: Removing Duplicates in Linked List
editorial.intuition: Iterate over the linked list and use a HashSet to track unique elements
editorial.bruteForce: Brute force approach involves iterating over the linked list and manually removing duplicates which is inefficient
editorial.betterSolution: Use a HashSet to track unique elements and iterate over the linked list
editorial.optimalSolution: Create a HashSet to track unique elements and iterate over the linked list. If an element is already in the set, skip it, otherwise add it to the set and the linked list
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The optimal solution ensures all unique elements are preserved in the linked list
editorial.dryRun: Run the optimal solution with the provided test case
commonMistakes: Not iterating over the entire linked list
Not using a HashSet to track unique elements
